steps.title: Find the sample proportion
steps.content: In Jordan's random sample of 25 employees, 20 are enrolled in exactly three professional development courses. The proportion is $$\frac{20}{25}=0.8.$$

steps.title: Scale to the full population
steps.content: Multiply the sample proportion by the total 500 employees: $$0.8\times 500=400.$$ So the best estimate is 400 employees.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) 5 — this is far too small; 5 is the number not enrolled in the sample, not a scaled estimate. Eliminate.
  • B) 400 — matches $0.8\times 500=400$. Keep.
  • C) 475 — would require a sample proportion of $475/500=0.95$, not $0.8$. Eliminate.
  • D) 480 — would require a sample proportion of $480/500=0.96$, not $0.8$. Eliminate.
That confirms B is correct.
